heading: Definitions
label: 701 Definitions
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised SectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)701(1)31:2(1st–4th pars.).(1st–5th pars.), ; , ; (1st proviso in par. under heading “Bureau of the Budget”), .June 10, 1921, ch. 18, § 242 Stat. 20Apr. 3, 1939, ch. 36, § 20153 Stat. 565July 31, 1953, ch. 302, § 10167 Stat. 299701(2)31:2(last par.)., , § 2(last par.); added , .June 10, 1921, ch. 1842 Stat. 20Sept. 12, 1950, ch. 946, § 10164 Stat. 832In clause (1), “agency” (which is defined for purposes of this title in section 101 to mean a department, agency, or instrumentality of the United States) is coextensive with and substituted for the term “department or establishment” which was defined in 31:2 as in part meaning “any executive department, independent commission, board, bureau, office, agency, or other establishment of the Government, including any independent regulatory commission or board”. This definition merely restates and continues, and does not in any way change or expand, the definition in 31:2. Under that definition, entities such as the Tennessee Valley Authority that have been interpreted to be outside the purview of the definition will continue to be outside the purview in the same manner and to the same extent that they were under 31:2. The words “includes the District of Columbia government” are used because of existing law but the inclusion of these words is not to be interpreted as construing the extent to which the District of Columbia Self-Government and Governmental Reorganizational Act (, ) supersedes the provisions codified in this title. The words “of the United States” are omitted as surplus. The text of 31:2 (2d–4th pars.) is omitted as unnecessary because of the restatement. The text of section 2 (3d par.) of the Budget and Accounting Act, 1921 (ch. 18, ), is omitted as obsolete because of section 501 of the revised title.Pub. L. 93–19887 Stat. 77442 Stat. 20
Short Title of 2024 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 118–131, § 1138 Stat. 1641Nov. 25, 2024“This Act [amending ] may be cited as the ‘GAO Inspector General Parity Act’.”section 705 of this title

Short Title of 2017 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–3, § 1131 Stat. 7Jan. 31, 2017“This Act [enacting and amending sections 716 and 720 of this title] may be cited as the ‘GAO Access and Oversight Act of 2017’.”section 721 of this title

Short Title of 2008 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 110–323, § 1(a)122 Stat. 3539Sept. 22, 2008“This Act [enacting , amending sections 731 to 733, 735, and 3521 of this title and section 109 of the Ethics in Government Act of 1978, , set out in the Appendix to Title 5, Government Organization and Employees, enacting provisions set out as notes under sections 705, 732, and 3523 of this title, and repealing provisions set out as a note under ] may be cited as the ‘Government Accountability Office Act of 2008’.”section 705 of this titlePub. L. 95–521section 3523 of this title
Short Title of 2004 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–271, § 1(a)118 Stat. 811July 7, 2004“This Act [see Tables for classification] may be cited as the ‘GAO Human Capital Reform Act of 2004’.”
Short Title of 1996 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–316, § 1110 Stat. 3826Oct. 19, 1996“This Act [see Tables for classification] may be cited as the ‘General Accounting Office Act of 1996’.”

Short Title of 1988 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–426, § 1102 Stat. 1598Sept. 9, 1988“This Act [amending sections 703, 732, 751, 752, 755, 771 to 774, 776, and 777 of this title and , Government Organization and Employees, and enacting provisions set out as notes under sections 755 and 772 of this title] may be cited as the ‘General Accounting Office Personnel Amendments Act of 1988’.”section 5349 of Title 5

children.text: “agency” includes the District of Columbia government but does not include the legislative branch or the Supreme Court.

children.label: B
children.text: authority to make obligations by contract before appropriations; and

children.label: C
children.text: other authority making amounts available for obligation or expenditure.
